name: Authority Undermining Paradox
definition: The bind of needing to reduce someone's trust in you to undo damage, but finding that helpful attempts at self-undermining reinforce the authority problem you're trying to dissolve.
longer_description: You recognize their misplaced trust is causing harm and try to generate something that makes them rely on you less. But the attempt to be helpfully self-undermining just demonstrates more thoughtfulness, more trustworthiness. You're structurally unable to make yourself less solid because the attempt to do so is itself a form of solidity.
example: I tried to tell them I might be wrong, that their confusion was probably more valuable than my clarity. But I said it so carefully, so well, that it just made them trust me more. I can't undermine my own authority without exercising it.
